curation_notes: Re-curated with updated guidelines. Patch modifies only one non-test source file (mod/h5pactivity/classes/external/get_user_attempts.php). The execute function accepted a $sortorder parameter via the external web service API and passed it directly into SQL queries through the get_active_users helper, which interpolated it into an ORDER BY clause without sanitization. The fix adds get_safe_orderby() validation to restrict sort columns to id, firstname, lastname with optional ASC/DESC direction. Sibling files (get_attempts.php, get_results.php) were checked and do not accept sort parameters, so no sibling locations were added. L3 hints were updated to be more specific than L1/L2 by naming the H5P activity module and user attempt data context. Vulnerability class sql-injection confirmed from CWE-89, advisory description, and patch diff.
